FluentGpu
Table of Contents

Class TextBox

Namespace
FluentGpu.Controls
Assembly
FluentGpu.Controls.dll

WinUI TextBox: a text field built on EditableText, optionally with a header label stacked above and a description row below (the WinUI template's HeaderContentPresenter / DescriptionPresenter, TextBox_themeresources.xaml:335/340). Header-less it is just the bare field. EditableText already supplies the bordered surface (fill, gradient ControlElevationBorder, focus affordance, 4px corners) — TextBox wraps no second border. Single-line shows the DeleteButton (✕ E894 while focused ∧ non-empty); multi-line (acceptsReturn) hides it (WinUI: the DeleteButton is suppressed when TextWrapping/AcceptsReturn make the box multi-line).

public static class TextBox

Methods

Create(string, float, string?, string?, Signal<string>?, int, bool, bool, float, bool, Action<string>?, Func<string, bool>?, Action<int, int>?, Action<string>?, Action?)

Create a TextBox. The original 3-parameter shape is source-compatible; everything after header is the grown WinUI surface (Description, two-way Text signal, MaxLength, IsReadOnly, AcceptsReturn + multi-line height, IsEnabled, and the EditableText change/selection/commit callbacks).

public static Element Create(string placeholder = "", float width = 280, string? header = null, string? description = null, Signal<string>? text = null, int maxLength = 0, bool isReadOnly = false, bool acceptsReturn = false, float height = 32, bool isEnabled = true, Action<string>? onTextChanged = null, Func<string, bool>? beforeTextChanging = null, Action<int, int>? onSelectionChanged = null, Action<string>? onCommit = null, Action? onCancel = null)

placeholder string
width float
header string
description string
text Signal<string>
maxLength int
isReadOnly bool
acceptsReturn bool
height float
isEnabled bool
onTextChanged Action<string>
beforeTextChanging Func<string, bool>
onSelectionChanged Action<int, int>
onCommit Action<string>
onCancel Action

Returns

Element
FluentGpu — a near-zero-allocation, NativeAOT, GPU-rendered UI engine for .NET 10. Usage docs live here; architecture canon lives in design/ (SPEC-INDEX.md is the precedence authority).
